raw_consumer_statement:
purchased a dresser from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX on XX/XX/year>. The product that arrived was not as describedthe actual length is about XXXX inches, not the advertised XXXX inches. I initiated a return through the merchants online portal on XX/XX/year>, which was well within the return timeframe. 

After initiating the return, the merchant did not provide the promised return instructions or a prepaid label. Instead, they required me to either pay {$110.00} for a return label or purchase a {$19.00} membership to be eligible for a free return. These conditions were not disclosed at the time of purchase and were added only after the return was initiated. 

The merchant repeatedly denied the dimensional discrepancy despite photographic proof and later confirmation from the manufacturer that the dresser length is XXXX inches. The merchant also repeatedly reset or altered the return date in their system and later claimed the return was past the policy timeframe, even though it was initiated on XX/XX/XXXX. 

The item is still in my possession only because the merchant refused to provide a return label or instructions. 

I submitted multiple disputes to my credit card issuer ( Citibank ), providing all evidence, including proof of timely return initiation, manufacturer confirmation, and records of merchant obstruction. The card issuer denied the dispute stating the timeframe has passed, even though the dispute was submitted within the FCBA/Reg Z timelines and the merchant clearly prevented completion of the return. 

All evidence is provided in the attached consolidated XXXX.
